questions.text: Which organelle would be more abundant in the secretory cell than the non secretory cell;
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Golgi apparatus is often regarded as the secretary organelle of the cell. This is because it helps in transporting protein within or outside the cell. It can either be transported from inside to outside or within the cell region.It is mainly used for the transportation of protein outside the cell.

questions.text: The structure which disappeared during cell division is:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Nucleolus and nuclear membrane always disappear during mitosis and meiosis. At the beginning of mitosis, the chromosomes condense, the nucleolus disappears, and the nuclear envelope breaks down, resulting in the release of most of the contents of the nucleus into the cytoplasm

questions.text: The enzyme ATP synthase is located on the membrane of the organelle:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: The ATP synthases comprise a very large group of highly conserved enzymes that are found in the bacterial cytoplasmic membranes, the thylakoid membranes of chloroplasts, and the inner membranes of mitochondria.

questions.text: _ are spherical sacs surrounded by a single membrane and containing hydrolytic enzymes:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: A lysosome is a membrane-bound organelle found in many animal cells. They are spherical vesicles that contain hydrolytic enzymes that can break down many kinds of biomolecules.

questions.text: Except during nuclear division, the nucleus has the chromosome in a loosely coiled state known as:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Chromatin are loosely coiled fibres of DNA and histone proteins present in the nucleus.

questions.text: Within the nucleus, _ are made by the nucleolus:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: The nucleolus is a spherical structure found in the cell's nucleus whose primary function is to produce and assemble the cell's ribosomes.

questions.text: Which of the following is correct for the cell wall:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: The cell wall is dead and fully permeable. It cannot regulate the entry of solvent or solute molecules. This fact is established by the accumulation of water and mineral salts in the periplasmic space, which is the space between the cell wall and the cell membrane. If the cell wall was semi or differentially permeable; mineral salts would not have accumulated in the periplasm.

questions.text: Why it is said that plasma membrane is asymmetrical:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Proteins are not fixed at their position
Hydrophobic tail facing inward and hydrophilic head facing outward
The two surfaces and halves are not identical
Cholesterol molecules are only present inside

questions.explanation: The cell membrane is an asymmetric structure. That means that the two sides of the membrane are structurally and functionally different.

questions.text: Cell membrane also contain _ through which movement of materials take place by and passive transport:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Channel proteins, gated channel proteins, and carrier proteins are three types of transport proteins that are involved in facilitated diffusion. A channel protein, a type of transport protein, acts like a pore in the membrane that lets water molecules or small ions through quickly.

questions.text: The _ part of plasma membrane controls the fluidity of the membrane:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: The amount of cholesterol in animal plasma membranes regulates the fluidity of the membrane and changes based on the temperature of the cell's environment. Cholesterol is one of several types of fats (lipids) that play an important role in your body.

questions.text: A group of ribosomes attached to mRNA is known as a polysome and the attachment is controlled by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The attachment of ribosomes to mRNA is controlled by Mg2+ ions.
The attachment of ribosomes to mRNA is controlled by Mg2+ ions. PTB- Pg 54
The attachment of ribosomes to mRNA is controlled by Mg2+ ions.
The attachment of ribosomes to mRNA is controlled by Mg2+ ions.

questions.explanation: Ribosomes are composed of two subunits known as large and small, which unites to form the active ribosome during the process of protein synthesis. The increased concentration of Mg2+leads to association of both the units while the decreased concentration favours the dissociation of the units.

questions.text: An automatic, involuntary response to any change. external or internal is caJled:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: An involuntary response to a stimulus is known as reflex action. Reflex actions occur when our body needs to react to immediate danger such as accidentally touching a hot object.

questions.text: Resting membrane potential measures:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: In most neurons the resting potential has a value of approximately −70 mV. The resting potential is mostly determined by the concentrations of the ions in the fluids on both sides of the cell membrane and the ion transport proteins that are in the cell membrane.

questions.text: Touch receptors are:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Meissner corpuscles consist of a cutaneous nerve ending responsible for transmitting the sensations of fine, discriminative touch and vibration.

questions.text: Which one is NOT a function of the large intestine:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Absorption of nutrients occurs in the small intestine. The nutrients are absorbed through the villi of the small intestine.

questions.text: The following part Is common in both the digestive system and respiratory systems of human beings:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: The pharynx is part of two body systems (the respiratory system and the digestive system) because it carries both air and food.

questions.text: Heartbeat is normally regulated by:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: The impulse starts in a small bundle of specialised cells located in the right atrium, called the SA node. The electrical activity spreads through the walls of the atria and causes them to contract. This forces blood into the ventricles. The SA node sets the rate and rhythm of your heartbeat.

questions.text: The method which helps in developing immunity against bacteria is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Radiotherapy is a medical treatment that uses ionizing radiation to kill cancer cells or shrink tumors. It is not used to develop immunity against bacteria; its primary purpose is cancer treatment.
Chemotherapy is a medical treatment that uses drugs to destroy cancer cells. Similar to radiotherapy, chemotherapy is not used to develop immunity against bacteria.

Vaccination, also known as immunization, is the correct option. It involves the administration of vaccines containing weakened or inactivated forms of bacteria or antigens to stimulate the immune system's production of antibodies. This helps the body develop immunity against specific bacteria or infectious diseases.

Antibiotics are medications used to treat bacterial infections by killing or inhibiting the growth of bacteria. While antibiotics are essential for treating bacterial infections, they do not help in developing long-term immunity against bacteria.

questions.explanation: Acquired immunity comes from vaccines and exposure to diseases. These enable the body to develop antigens that can help it fight the same disease a second time.

questions.text: Normal gestation period in humans is about _ days
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: The average length of human gestation is 280 days, or 40 weeks, from the first day of the woman's last menstrual period.

questions.text: During tho menstruaI cycle, after the female gemete is released from the ovary is ovulation, the remains of the follicle secrets:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: As the egg migrates down the fallopian tube, progesterone is released. It is secreted by a temporary gland formed within the ovary after ovulation called the corpus luteum. Progesterone prepares the body for pregnancy by causing the uterine lining to thicken. If a woman is not pregnant, the corpus luteum disappears.

questions.text: In the male reproductive system, the hormone involved in the regulation of the rate of spermatogenesis is called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: LH is also produced by the anterior pituitary gland and is involved in regulating the production of testosterone in the testes. It stimulates Leydig cells in the testes to produce testosterone, which is essential for various male reproductive functions.

In the male reproductive system, the hormone involved in the regulation of the rate of spermatogenesis (sperm production) is Follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH) (option b). FSH is produced and released by the anterior pituitary gland and plays a crucial role in the development and maturation of sperm cells.

Testosterone is the primary male sex hormone produced by the testes. It plays a crucial role in the development of male reproductive tissues, including the testes and prostate, as well as in the promotion of secondary sexual characteristics.

nhibin is produced by the Sertoli cells in the testes and has a negative feedback effect on the anterior pituitary. It helps regulate the secretion of FSH by inhibiting its production when the sperm count is sufficient.

questions.explanation: The hormone involved in the regulation of the rate of spermatogenesis in the male reproductive system is Follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH) (option b). It stimulates the development and maturation of sperm cells in the testes.

questions.text: Cervix is the pan of:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: The cervix is the lower, narrow part of the uterus (womb). The cervix connects the main body of the uterus to the vagina (birth canal).

questions.text: Which of the following is most abundant in human body:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Hyaline cartilage is a glossy, greyish-white tissue with a uniform appearance. It is one of the three types of cartilage; the other two types are elastic cartilage and fibrocartilage. Hyaline cartilage is the most abundant type of cartilage in the body.

questions.text: Which of the following is an example of a joint called suture:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: The bones of the skull are an example. The immovable joints between the plates of the skull are known as sutures. Amphiarthrosis (slightly movable). Also known as cartilaginous joints, these joints are defined as two or more bones held so tightly together that only limited movement can take place.

questions.text: Which event does not occur during muscle contraction:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: During muscle contraction, the I-band (isotropic band), which contains only thin actin filaments, shortens as the thin filaments slide toward the center of the sarcomere.

During muscle contraction, several structural changes occur within the sarcomere, which is the basic functional unit of a muscle fiber. However, the A-band (anisotropic band), which contains the thick myosin filaments, does not significantly change in length during muscle contraction. The A-band remains relatively constant in size as the myosin filaments slide past the actin filaments.

The H-zone is a region within the A-band where only myosin filaments are present. During muscle contraction, the H-zone narrows or disappears entirely as the myosin filaments interact with the actin filaments and the sarcomere shortens.

Z-lines are structures that define the boundaries of the sarcomere. During muscle contraction, the Z-lines move closer together as the sarcomere shortens due to the sliding of thin actin filaments over thick myosin filaments.

questions.explanation: Among the given options, the event that does not occur during muscle contraction is b) A-band shortens. The A-band remains relatively constant in length as the myosin filaments slide past the actin filaments.

questions.text: Smooth muscles are long and spindle-shaped with _ nucleus per cell:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Smooth muscle cells are spindle shaped, have a single, centrally located nucleus, and lack striations. They are called involuntary muscles.

questions.text: Blood group which is known as a universal donor is:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: O negative donors are often called 'universal donors' because anyone can receive the red blood cells from their donations. Although about 8% of the population has O negative blood, it accounts for around 13% of hospital requests for red blood cells.

questions.text: Which one can be considered as living characteristic of a virus:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Living characteristics of viruses include the ability to reproduce – but only in living host cells – and the ability to mutate.

questions.text: A group of organisms with similar morphology and physiology. which can breed together to produce fertile offspring is called:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Species is defined as a group of organisms capable of interbreeding and creating fertile offspring.

questions.text: If oxygen is available, the complete breakdown of glucose produces _ ATP molecules in proka,yotos.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: A total of 38 ATP molecules (2 ATP molecules from glycolysis, 2 from the Krebs cycle, and 34 from the electron transport system) are produced per molecule of glucose oxidized in aerobic prokaryotes. Hence, this option is incorrect.

A total of 38 ATP molecules (2 ATP molecules from glycolysis, 2 from the Krebs cycle, and 34 from the electron transport system) are produced per molecule of glucose oxidized in aerobic prokaryotes. Hence, this option is incorrect.
A total of 38 ATP molecules (2 ATP molecules from glycolysis, 2 from the Krebs cycle, and 34 from the electron transport system) are produced per molecule of glucose oxidized in aerobic prokaryotes.

A total of 38 ATP molecules (2 ATP molecules from glycolysis, 2 from the Krebs cycle, and 34 from the electron transport system) are produced per molecule of glucose oxidized in aerobic prokaryotes. Hence, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: A total 38 ATP molecules (2 ATP molecules from glycolysis, 2 from Krebs cycle and 34 from electron transport system) are produced per molecule of glucose oxidised in aerobic prokaryotes.

questions.text: What are the products of light dependent reactions of photosynthesis?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: The three products of the light reaction of photosynthesis are ATP, NADPH, and O2

questions.text: In the process of photosynthesis water act as:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Water also provides electrons that bind to the hydrogen atoms to the carbon to produce glucose. Water also acts as a reducing agent which reduces NADP+ to NADPH by providing H+ ions. NADPH activates chlorophyll that in turn provides the green pigment to the plant.

questions.text: During photosynthesis, CO2 works as:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: During photosynthesis, plants take in carbon dioxide (CO2) and water (H2O) from the air and soil. Within the plant cell, the water is oxidised, meaning it loses electrons, while the carbon dioxide is reduced, meaning it gains electrons. This transforms the water into oxygen and the carbon dioxide into glucose.

questions.text: End product of Calvin cycle Is:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: In the second stage, ATP and NADPH are used to convert the 3-PGA molecules into molecules of a three-carbon sugar, glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate (G3P).

questions.text: Which of the following molecule contains amino acid:?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Collagen molecules contain amino acids.

questions.text: Hydrolysis is the breakdown of Polymer into its monomers by the addition of:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Polymers are broken down into monomers via hydrolysis reactions, in which a bond is broken, or lysed, by addition of a water molecule.

questions.text: Which of the following is not a carbohydrate:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Lactic acid is an organic molecule. Its chemical formula is C3H6O3 Lactic acid has the same proportion of carbon, hydrogen and oxygen as carbohydrates but it is technically not a carbohydrate.

questions.text: Due to its high heat of vaporization water plays important role in living organism as:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Due to its high heat capacity, water is used by warm blooded animals to more evenly disperse heat in their bodies; it acts in a similar manner to a car's cooling system, transporting heat from warm places to cool places, causing the body to maintain a more even temperature.

questions.text: Which molecules do not contribute to the formation of biological membranes?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: The nucleoprotein of negative-strand RNA viruses forms a major component of the ribonucleoprotein complex that is responsible for viral transcription and replication.

questions.text: Which of the following molecules would yield glucose and fructose on hydrolysis?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: When sucrose is hydrolyzed, one molecule each of α D -glucose and β D fructose.

questions.text: The terminal ends of the chromosomes are called:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: A telomere is a region of repetitive nucleotide sequences at each end of a chromatid, which protects the end of the chromosome from deterioration or from fusion with neighbouring chromosomes.

questions.text: The gap between neurons at a synapse is:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: A synapse is the small gap between two neurons, where nerve impulses are relayed by a neurotransmitter from the axon of a presynaptic (sending) neuron to the dendrite of a postsynaptic (receiving) neuron. It is referred to as the synaptic cleft or synaptic gap.

questions.text: The part of the brain which controls the body temperature in human beings is called:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Our internal body temperature is regulated by a part of our brain called the hypothalamus. The hypothalamus checks our current temperature and compares it with the normal temperature of about 37°C.

questions.text: A long extension of a nerve cell is called:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: An extension or process called an axon carries impulses away from the cell body.

questions.text: Which is a steroid hormone?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: The steroids that are made almost exclusively in the adrenal glands are cortisol, 11-deoxycortisol, aldosterone, corticosterone, and 11-deoxycorti-costerone. Most other steroid hormones, including the estrogens, are made by the adrenal glands and the gonads.

questions.text: The molecules responsible for conveying messages from one neuron to the next are called:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Neurotransmitters are chemical compounds released by neurons after depolarization that act on other neurons to produce a response.

questions.text: Which enzyme works in alkaline pH?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: The function of the pancreatic juice is to digest and break down large sugars, fats, and starches into smaller assimilable molecules. These enzymes are alkaline. Hence, their optimum pH level is 7-14. The alkaline pH helps to activate the pancreatic enzymes secreted by the acinar cells.

questions.text: Considering enzyme action minimum temperature is the term used when:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Minimum temperature is the term used when an inactive enzyme starts getting reactive.

questions.text: Which option is correct about non-competitive inhibition
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: A noncompetitive inhibitor binds to the enzyme away from the active site, altering the shape of the enzyme so that even if the substrate can bind, the active site functions less effectively.

questions.text: Which type of enzyme would be most active in your gut after eating the loaf of bread?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Amylase is produced mostly in the pancreas, but also in the salivary glands and small intestine. One type of amylase, called ptyalin, is made in the salivary glands and starts to act on starches while food is still in your mouth. It remains active even after you swallow.

questions.text: He presented the theory of "origin of species by means of natural selection":
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: The theory of natural selection was explored by 19th-century naturalist Charles Darwin. Natural selection explains how genetic traits of a species may change over time. This may lead to speciation, the formation of a distinct new species.

questions.text: Which is not a mode of action of an antibody?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Antibody cells do not secrete many cytokines upon activation.

questions.text: Magnesium is an important nutrient in plants for the formation of:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Magnesium is a key component of chlorophyll, the green colouring material of plants, and is vital for photosynthesis.

questions.text: Which of the following parts of the respiratory system has no cartilage?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Terminal Bronchioles is the last part of the conducting portion of the respiratory system, and has the smallest diameter of all (less than 1mm). There is no cartilage, or glands, some smooth muscle is still present, there are no goblet cells. The epithelium is either columnar or cuboidal.

questions.text: Enzymatic portion of the gastric juices secreted by which mucosal calls?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Gastric chief cells secrete pepsin as an inactive zymogen called pepsinogen.

questions.text: Tho horizontal distance travellled by wave during one complete cycle Is called
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The wavelength (λ) is the total distance travelled by the wave in one full cycle. In this case, the wavelength can be given by horizontal distance between repeating units of the wave pattern.

questions.text: If the period of oscillation of mass (M) suspended from a spring is 1s, then the period of mass 16M will be
questions.subject: Physics

questions.text: Loudness of the sound is directly related to
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The loudness of a sound is directly related to its intensity. Intensity is a measure of the amount of sound energy passing through a unit area per unit of time. The greater the intensity of a sound wave, the louder it appears to our ears. Mathematically, the relationship between loudness and intensity can be expressed as: Loudness ∝ Intensity In this equation, the symbol "∝" means "is directly proportional to." It means that as the intensity of the sound wave increases, the loudness also increases, and vice versa. It's important to note that our perception of loudness is not solely determined by intensity. Other factors, such as the frequency or pitch of the sound, also influence how loud a sound is perceived to be. The relationship between loudness and intensity, however, remains a significant factor in understanding how the human ear interprets and responds to different sound levels.
Since the loudness of a sound is directly related to its intensity. Hence this option is incorrect.
Since the loudness of a sound is directly related to its intensity. Hence this option is incorrect.
Since the loudness of a sound is directly related to its intensity. Hence this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The loudness of a sound relates the intensity of any given sound to the intensity at the threshold of hearing. It is measured in decibels (dB). The threshold of human hearing has an intensity of about . 0000000000001 watts per metre squared and corresponds to 0 decibels.

questions.text: The increase in the speed of sound in air for each degree rise above 0oC is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is factual recall correct answer is 0.61m/s
This is factual recall correct answer is 0.61m/s
This is factual recall correct answer is 0.61m/s
This is factual recall correct answer is 0.61m/s

questions.explanation: The speed of sound is about 331.5 m/s at 0 degree-Celsius. The speed of sound increases by 0.6 meters per second (m/s) for every degree-Celsius increase in temperature.

questions.text: First law of thermodynamics is based on
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The first law of thermodynamics is based on the law of conservation of energy, which states that energy cannot be created or destroyed, but can be transferred from one form to another

questions.text: The thermodynamic process during which the pressure is kept constant is called
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: In thermodynamics, an isochoric process, also called a constant-volume process, an isovolumetric process, or an isometric process, is a thermodynamic process during which the volume of the closed system undergoing such a process remains constant.

In thermodynamics, an adiabatic process is a type of thermodynamic process that occurs without transferring heat or mass between the thermodynamic system and its environment. Unlike an isothermal process, an adiabatic process transfers energy to the surroundings only as work.

An isobaric process is a process occurring at constant pressure. The first law of thermodynamic equation for the isobaric process remains the same as the pressure remains constant and because of the volume change, the system does work.
In thermodynamics, an isothermal process is a type of thermodynamic process in which the temperature T of a system remains constant: ΔT = 0.

questions.explanation: An Isobaric process is a thermodynamic process taking place at constant pressure.

questions.text: Energy stored in a capacitor is given by
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The energy stored in a capacitor can be expressed in three ways: E=((CV)2)/2 where Q is the charge, V is the voltage, and C is the capacitance of the capacitor. The energy is in joules for a charge in coulombs, voltage in volts, and capacitance in farads.

questions.text: Electric field intensity is
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: A measure of the force exerted by one charged body on another. Imaginary lines of force or electric field lines originate (by convention) on positive charges and terminate on negative charges.

questions.text: Which of the following stores electrical potential energy?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Unlike the battery, a capacitor is a circuit component that temporarily stores electrical energy through distributing charged particles on (generally two) plates to create a potential difference. A capacitor can take a shorter time than a battery to charge up and it can release all the energy very quickly.

A conductor, or electrical conductor, is a substance or material that allows electricity to flow through it. In a conductor, electrical charge carriers, usually electrons or ions, move easily from atom to atom when voltage is applied.

An inductor, also called a coil, choke, or reactor, is a passive two-terminal electrical component that stores energy in a magnetic field when electric current flows through it. An inductor typically consists of an insulated wire wound into a coil.

A generator is a device that converts mechanical energy into electrical energy. It is based on the principle of electromagnetic induction, which was discovered by Michael Faraday in the 1830s. Generators play a crucial role in various applications, including power generation, providing electricity in homes and industries, and serving as backup power sources during emergencies.

questions.explanation: The energy stored between the plates of a charged capacitor is electrical potential energy.

questions.text: A magnitude of the current an metals is proportional to the applied voltage as long as temperature of conductor is kept constant. It is statement of
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Ohm's law states that the current through a conductor between two points is directly proportional to the voltage across the two points as long as temperature of the conductor is kept constant.

questions.text: The resistance of pure metal increases with
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: With the increase in temperature, the random motion of electrons increases. As a result, the number of collisions of electrons with the positive ions increases in a metal. Hence, the resistance of a metal increases with increase in temperature.

questions.text: The magnetic field inside the current carrying wire varies
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: To find the magnetic field at a radius r inside the wire, draw a circular loop of radius r. The magnetic field should still go in circular loops, just as it does outside the wire. So, inside the wire the magnetic field is proportional to r, while outside it's proportional to 1/r.

questions.text: Magnetic flux is maximum when angle between magnetic field and vector area is
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Magnetic flux is maximum when the angle between the magnetic field and the normal to the plane of finite area is 00

questions.text: Lenz's law os based on the law of conservation of
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Lenz's law is based on the law of conservation of energy. From the definition of Lenz's law, we know that the induced current is always opposed by the cause that produces it. Therefore, there is extra work done against the opposing force.

questions.text: In fleming's right hand rule, the thumb indicates
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Fleming's right-hand rule gives which direction the current flows. The right hand is held with the thumb, index finger and middle finger mutually perpendicular to each other (at right angles), as shown in the diagram. The thumb is pointed in the direction of the motion of the conductor relative to the magnetic field.

questions.text: Transformer works on the principle of
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The transformer works on the principle of Faraday's law of electromagnetic induction and mutual induction. There are usually two coils: primary coil and secondary coil on the transformer core.

questions.text: A device that converts AC into DC is called
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Rectifier is a device that is used to convert AC voltage into DC voltage and is called a rectifier.

questions.text: The conversion of alternating current into direct current is called
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: A rectifier converts alternating current to direct current and this process of converting AC to DC is known as rectification.

questions.text: 1 Gy is equal to
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: One Gy is the international system of units (SI) equivalent to 100 rads, which is equal to an absorbed dose of 1 Joule/kilogram.

questions.text: A 32g radioactive element decays and remains 2g after 60 days. What is the half- life of radioactive element?
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The time taken for half of the original population of radioactive atoms to decay is called the half-life. 32/2=16 16/2=8 8/2=4 4/2=2 60/4=15 days

questions.text: An elastic collision is the one In which:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Kinetic energy and momentum is conserved
Kinetic energy is conserved but total energy is not conserved
Momentum is conserved but kinetic energy is not conserved
Both kinetic energy and momentum are not conserved

questions.explanation: An elastic collision is a collision in which there is no net loss in kinetic energy in the system as a result of the collision. Both momentum and kinetic energy are conserved quantities in elastic collisions.

questions.text: The time rate of change of velocity Is called
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The rate of change in velocity is called acceleration.

questions.text: In projectile motion, the rage of projectile will be maximum at an angle of
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Since 45 degrees is the correct answer, hence this option is incorrect.
The range of a projectile for a given initial velocity is maximum when the angle of projection is 45∘.
Since 45 degrees is the correct answer, hence this option is incorrect.
Since 45 degrees is the correct answer, hence this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The range of a projectile for a given initial velocity is maximum when the angle of projection is 450

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT TRUE?
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Action and reaction have same nature
options.explanation: Action and reaction act on two different bodies, hence this option is incorrect.

options.text: Action and reaction have same line of action
options.explanation: Action and reaction act on two different bodies, hence this option is incorrect.

options.text: Action and reaction never act on same body
options.explanation: Action and reaction can act on the same body. Newton's third law states that when two bodies interact, they apply forces to one another that are equal in magnitude and opposite in direction. The third law is also known as the law of action and reaction. Since this statement is not true hence this option is correct.

options.text: Action and reaction can cancel each other
options.explanation: Either force can be considered the action force or the reaction force. You might think that because action-reaction forces are equal and opposite that they cancel. However, action and reaction force pairs don't cancel because they act on different objects. Forces can cancel only if they act on the same object.

questions.explanation: Either force can be considered the action force or the reaction force. You might think that because action-reaction forces are equal and opposite that they cancel. However, action and reaction force pairs don't cancel because they act on different objects. Forces can cancel only if they act on the same object.

questions.text: Two buses moving at 100 km/h and 80 km/h respectively cross each other while moving in opposite direction. Velocity of one bus relative to other bus is
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Here the two buses are moving in the opposite directions Their speeds are u=100 km/hr and v=80 km/hr ∴ Relative speed =(u+v) km/hr =(100+80)=180 km/hr Hence, the buses have their relative speeds as 180 km/hr.

questions.text: Which of the following pair of angles have same rang for a projectile?
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: 75 degrees and 15 degrees will have the same range for a projectile.

questions.text: The angle of projection of a projectile for which its maximum height and horizontal range are equal is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: If the angle of projection is 76 degrees, the maximum height is equal to the horizontal range.
If the angle of projection is 76 degrees, the maximum height is equal to the horizontal range.
If the angle of projection is 76 degrees, the maximum height is equal to the horizontal range.
If the angle of projection is 76 degrees, the maximum height is equal to the horizontal range.

questions.explanation: If the angle of projection is 76 degrees, the maximum height is equal to the horizontal range.

questions.text: The explosion of explosive material is application of
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: This is due to the fact that momentum is conserved for all interactions, including collisions and explosions. However, kinetic energy is only preserved in elastic interactions, which an explosion is most obviously not. An explosion maintains the Law of Conservation of Momentum.

questions.text: lhp is equal to
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: One mechanical horsepower of 550 foot-pounds per second is equivalent to 745.7 watts.

questions.text: The product of force and velocity Is equal to
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The product of force and velocity is power.

questions.text: One kilowatt-hour is equal to

questions.text: The amount of work done in moving a body at certain point in a gravitational field to a position of zero potential such that the body Is never accelerated is called
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The work done in moving a body from a certain point in a gravitational field to a position of zero potential without acceleration is called gravitational potential. Gravitational potential energy is defined in the textbook as the amount of work done in bringing a mass from infinity to that point in gravitational field without producing acceleration

questions.text: If the speed of a body is doubled, its kinetic energy becomes:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: If the speed of a body is doubled, its kinetic energy becomes four times its original value. Hence, this option is incorrect.
If the speed of a body is doubled, its kinetic energy becomes four times its original value. Hence, this option is incorrect.

If the speed of a body is doubled, its kinetic energy becomes four times its original value. The kinetic energy (KE) of an object is given by the formula: KE = (1/2) * m * v^2 Where: KE = Kinetic energy m = Mass of the object v = Velocity (speed) of the object When the speed (v) of the body is doubled (let's call it 2v), the kinetic energy becomes: New KE = (1/2) * m * (2v)^2 New KE = (1/2) * m * 4v^2 New KE = 2 * (1/2) * m * v^2 New KE = 2 * KE As you can see, the kinetic energy becomes twice the original value. Therefore, when the speed of a body is doubled, its kinetic energy becomes four times its original value { 4 x (1/2) mv2= 2 mv2 }

questions.explanation: If the speed of a body is doubled, its kinetic energy becomes four times its original value. The kinetic energy (KE) of an object is given by the equation: KE = (1/2) * m * v^2 Where m is the mass of the object and v is its velocity (speed). When the speed of the body is doubled, we can express the new speed as 2v (twice the original velocity). Substituting the new speed into the equation for kinetic energy, we have: KE_new = (1/2) * m * (2v)^2 = (1/2) * m * 4v^2 = 4 * (1/2) * m * v^2 = 4 *KE-original Therefore, when the speed of a body is doubled, its kinetic energy becomes four times its original value { 4 x (1/2) mv2 = 2 mv2 }.

questions.text: Choose the correctly structured sentence.
questions.subject: English

options.text: Mr. Shan. with his family together goes to England.
options.explanation: The sentence you provided is almost correct but requires a minor adjustment for proper grammar: "Mr. Shan, together with his family, goes to England." Alternatively, you could write: "Mr. Shan goes to England with his family." Both versions are acceptable, but the first one includes the phrase "together with his family" to emphasize that Mr. Shan is going to England with his family.

options.text: Mr. Shan, together with his family, goes to England.
options.explanation: The sentence "Mr. Shan, together with his family, goes to England." is grammatically correct. In this sentence, the phrase "together with his family" is set off by commas, indicating additional information about Mr. Shan. The subject of the sentence is "Mr. Shan," and the verb is "goes," which agrees with the singular subject. Overall, the sentence effectively conveys that Mr. Shan, along with his family, is going to England.

options.text: Mr. Shan. together with his farni y, go to England.
options.explanation: The sentence "Mr. Shan. together with his family, go to England." still contains an error. Let's correct it: "Mr. Shan, together with his family, goes to England." In this corrected version, the subject "Mr. Shan" is singular, so the verb should be "goes" to agree with the singular subject. The phrase "together with his family" is set off by commas, providing additional information about Mr. Shan, and the sentence now conveys that he, along with his family, is going to England.

options.text: Mr. Shan, with his family, go to England logether
options.explanation: The sentence "Mr. Shan, with his family, go to England together" contains a subject-verb agreement error. The correct version should be: "Mr. Shan, with his family, goes to England together." In this corrected version, the subject "Mr. Shan" is singular, so the verb should be "goes" to agree with the singular subject. The phrase "with his family" provides additional information about Mr. Shan, and the sentence now conveys that he, along with his family, is going to England together.

questions.explanation: Mr. Shan, together with his family, goes to England.

questions.text: Geometric isomerism is exhibited by:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Geometric Isomerism is commonly observed in Carbon-Carbon double bonds. Carbon-carbon double bonded compounds have restricted rotation. Geometric isomers can occur where there is restricted rotation about a bond. Hence, alkenes exhibit geometric isomerism.

questions.text: Cinnazzaro's reaction is given by:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The Cannizzaro reaction is basically a chemical reaction which involves the base-induced disproportionation of an aldehyde lacking a hydrogen atom in the alpha position or an alpha-hydrogen. Formaldehyde (HCHO) has no alpha hydrogen, so it can undergo Cannizzaro reaction.

questions.text: The identification test for Ketone is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Benedict's solution is used to test for the presence of reducing sugars, such as glucose. It is not specific to ketones and does not provide information about the presence of ketone functional groups.
Fehling's solution is also used to test for reducing sugars, particularly aldehyde functional groups. Similar to Benedict's solution, it is not specific to ketones.

The Sodium Nitroprusside Test is specifically used to identify the presence of ketone functional groups in organic compounds. It involves a color change reaction that confirms the presence of ketones.
Town's test is used to detect the presence of phenolic compounds. It involves the formation of a blue or green color when a phenol reacts with sodium hydroxide and a halogen.

questions.explanation: The identification test for ketones involves the Sodium Nitroprusside Test. This test is used to detect the presence of ketone functional groups in organic compounds. When a compound containing a ketone group reacts with Sodium Nitroprusside in the presence of a base, a characteristic color change occurs, typically from colorless to purple.

questions.text: Carboxylic acids are water soluble because they:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: When added to water the carboxylic acids do not form dimers. Rather, hydrogen bonds are formed between the individual molecules of the acid and water molecules. It is because of these interactions that carboxylic acids can dissolve in water to form acidic solutions.

questions.text: Which of the following represents equation for ideal gas?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The ideal gas equation is formulated as: PV = nRT. In this equation, P refers to the pressure of the ideal gas, V is the volume of the ideal gas, n is the total amount of ideal gas that is measured in terms of moles, R is the universal gas constant, and T is the temperature.

questions.text: How should the condition be changed to prevent the volume of a given gas from expanding when its mass is increased?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Temperature is lowered & pressure is increased
options.explanation: Lowering the temperature reduces the kinetic energy of gas molecules, causing them to move more slowly and exert less pressure on the container walls. Increasing the pressure compresses the gas, reducing its volume. This combination of lower temperature and higher pressure counteracts the expansion caused by an increase in gas mass, keeping the volume relatively constant.

options.text: Temperature is increased & pressure is lowered
options.explanation: Increasing the temperature of a gas results in higher kinetic energy of gas molecules, causing them to exert more pressure on the container walls. Lowering the pressure allows the gas to expand more freely. This combination encourages the expansion of the gas when its mass is increased, rather than preventing it.
Lowering both temperature and pressure would not prevent the gas from expanding when its mass increases. In fact, this would likely promote further contraction of the gas.
Increasing both temperature and pressure would not prevent the gas from expanding when its mass increases. Instead, it would likely promote further expansion.

questions.explanation: To prevent the volume of a given gas from expanding when its mass is increased, you should lower the temperature and increase the pressure (option a). This combination of conditions counteracts the expansion and keeps the volume relatively constant.

questions.text: Which variable mentioned in ideal gas is assumed to be constant in other gas laws?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: In various gas laws, volume can be a variable that changes. For example, Boyle's law (P1V1 = P2V2) specifically deals with the relationship between pressure and volume at constant temperature and moles, allowing volume to change.

Temperature is another variable that can change in gas laws. Charles's law (V1/T1 = V2/T2) focuses on the relationship between volume and temperature at constant pressure and moles, demonstrating that temperature can vary.

Pressure is a variable that can change in gas laws as well. Boyle's law (P1V1 = P2V2) emphasizes the relationship between pressure and volume at constant temperature and moles, illustrating that pressure is not necessarily constant.

The number of moles (n) is often assumed to be constant in other gas laws when using ideal gas behavior. This assumption simplifies the relationships studied in these laws. The ideal gas law, PV = nRT, incorporates all these variables (pressure, volume, temperature, and moles) and is versatile for various gas law scenarios.

questions.explanation: While each of these variables (volume, temperature, pressure) can change in different gas laws, the number of moles (n) is generally assumed to be constant when applying ideal gas behavior in these laws. The ideal gas law itself, PV = nRT, encompasses all these variables and their relationships, making it a comprehensive equation for gas behavior.

questions.text: Which of tho following molecules will showe higher rate of evaporation?

questions.text: Which term describes a solution in which dissolved solute is in equilibrium with undissolved solute?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: A solution in which the maximum amount of solvent has been dissolved. Any more solute added will sit as crystals on the bottom of the container.

questions.text: Rate of any reaction depends upon:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Increasing the temperature generally increases the rate of a reaction. Higher temperatures provide more kinetic energy to the reactant molecules, leading to more frequent and energetic collisions, which in turn leads to a higher reaction rate

The concentration of reactants affects the rate of reaction. Higher concentrations of reactants mean more molecules available for collisions, increasing the likelihood of effective collisions and speeding up the reaction rate.

Pressure primarily affects gas-phase reactions. Increasing the pressure for gaseous reactants can lead to higher concentrations of reactant molecules, which can influence the reaction rate according to the principles mentioned above.

The rate constant (k) is a proportionality constant in the rate equation that relates the rate of reaction to the concentrations of reactants. The value of the rate constant is specific to each reaction and depends on temperature and other factors. It influences the rate of reaction according to the rate equation.

questions.explanation: The rate of reaction depends on the concentration of reactants, temperature, pressure (in case of gaseous reactants), and catalyst. All of these factors are interconnected and influence the rate of a reaction. Therefore, the correct answer is that the rate of any reaction depends on all of the above factors (temperature, concentration, pressure, and rate constant).

questions.text: What is " NOT TRUE" about rate of a reaction?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Concentration of reactants does not affect the rate of a reaction
Surface area of reactants affects rate of reaction
Catalyst affects rate of a reaction

questions.explanation: The rate constant which is the proportionality constant that relates the rate of reaction with the concentrations of the reactants is not dependent on the concentration of the reactants.

questions.text: Enthalpy change of combustion is exothermic becauase:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Energy released in bond breaking is greater
Energy released in bond making is greater
Energy absorbed in bond breaking is greater
Energy absorbed In bond making is greater

questions.explanation: Breaking these bonds requires energy to be absorbed by the reaction. But then, new bonds form between hydrogen and oxygen and between carbon and oxygen. For this reaction, the energy released is larger than the energy absorbed. This means combustion has an overall negative enthalpy and is an exothermic reaction.

questions.text: During electrolysis, reduction always occurs at:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Oxidation happens at the positive anode because this is where negative ions lose electrons.
Reduction happens at the negative cathode because this is where positive ions gain electrons.
It depends on the electrode attached to SHE that whether reduction or oxidation will occur on SHE.
Salt bridge makes contact between two half cells and allows passage of ions between them.

questions.explanation: Reduction happens at the negative cathode because this is where positive ions gain electrons. Oxidation happens at the positive anode because this is where negative ions lose electrons.

questions.text: Type of hybridization of carbon in Ethene (H2C=CH2) is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: sp3 hybridization involves the combination of one 2s orbital and three 2p orbitals to form four equivalent sp3 hybrid orbitals. This type of hybridization is typically found in molecules with tetrahedral geometry, where carbon forms four sigma bonds

sp hybridization involves the combination of one 2s orbital and one 2p orbital to form two equivalent sp hybrid orbitals. This type of hybridization is found in molecules with linear geometry, where carbon forms two sigma bonds.

dsp2 hybridization is a type of hybridization involving one d orbital, one s orbital, and two p orbitals. It is not applicable to the carbon atoms in ethene, which are best described by sp2 hybridization due to the trigonal planar arrangement of the bonds around the carbon atoms.

questions.explanation: The number of monovalent atoms attached to C is two. This suggests that C in ethylene (H₂C=CH₂) is sp² hybridised. Thus the carbon-carbon bond in ethylene is formed by the overlapping of the sp² hybrid orbitals of carbon atoms.

questions.text: Which of the following molecule is polar?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: HCl is polar covalent compound as the chloride ion is more electronegative than hydrogen ion. So chloride ions carry partial negative character while hydrogen carries partial positive character. Covalent character is shown by HCl as the atoms of hydrogen and chlorine share their electrons with each other.

questions.text: Electron affinity decreases down the group because:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: While proton number (atomic number) increases down a group, this does not directly influence the trend in electron affinity. The primary factor affecting electron affinity down a group is the change in atomic radius.

Shielding, also known as electron shielding or electron screening, refers to the repulsion between electrons in different electron shells. It does play a role in atomic properties, but its effect on the trend in electron affinity is secondary compared to the influence of atomic radius.
This statement is incorrect. The trend in electron affinity does not correlate with a decrease in atomic radius down a group; rather, it correlates with an increase in atomic radius.

questions.explanation: As atomic size increases, the electrons become further away from the nucleus and hence, electron affinity decreases. Another factor which affects electron affinity is effective nuclear charge, which is defined as the positive force experienced by electrons due to the pull of the positively charged nucleus.
